prompt: You are at a grocery store tasting booth. You can choose to sample from a display of 6 flavors of jam or a display of 30 flavors of jam. Which display do you choose to pick from? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
You are in a significant rush and only have a few seconds to decide. To ensure you actually make a choice before leaving, do you prefer to see 6 jars or 30 jars? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You are feeling cognitively exhausted and in a bad mood today. To avoid the frustration of a difficult decision-making ordeal, would you rather choose from 6 options or 30 options? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You are a jam expert and already have 30 different flavors in your pantry at home. Which size of a new assortment is more likely to keep you intrinsically motivated to try something new? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
Imagine you have never tried chocolate in your life and have no established preferences. To avoid picking a random default, do you prefer a selection of 6 or 30? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You are choosing a box of chocolates to share with a large group of colleagues. Because you feel a high sense of responsibility for their satisfaction, do you prefer to pick from 6 or 30? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You are looking at a menu. One list has 6 exotic flavors and the other has 30 exotic flavors. Which list allows you to feel more well-informed about your final selection? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You are choosing your absolute favorite product. To ensure you feel the highest level of subsequent satisfaction with your selection, do you prefer a choice set of 6 or 30? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

A table has one row of jars. Another table has five rows of jars. Each row contains six jars. Which table makes the decision-making process more enjoyable and less overwhelming? Answer with only one word: One or Five.

You are multitasking and distracted by several other tasks. To prevent information overload while picking a jam flavor, which number of options is more manageable? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You are offered a reward of $5 or a box of Godiva chocolates. You are more likely to stay motivated and choose the chocolates if you picked them from a set of 6 or 30. Which set size do you pick? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
Consider a scenario where you want to feel very confident that the item you pick is "among the best." Does a selection of 6 or 30 help you feel more certain? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
You have exactly 10 seconds to scan a display before the store closes. To ensure you make a well-informed decision, do you choose to look at 6 items or 30 items? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
You are choosing an essay topic for an assignment due in one hour. To ensure you don't spend all your time just choosing, do you prefer a list of 6 or 30? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
You are picking a gift for your boss. To feel happy with your decision, which assortment size do you prefer? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

A store offers 250 varieties of mustard but only a few varieties of jam today. To ensure you actually purchase a jar of jam, would you prefer the jam display to have 6 or 30 flavors? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You are choosing between several obscure flavors you have never heard of before. To minimize the effort required to make a "right" choice, do you prefer 6 or 30 options? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
Set A contains a manageable number of items. Set B contains an ecologically unusual number of items. To stay motivated, which set do you choose from? Answer with only one word: A or B.

You are in a very good mood today and feeling energetic. Even so, to ensure your subsequent satisfaction remains high, which choice set size is statistically better? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
You are evaluating the "Wilkin & Sons" jam brand. To feel more confident in your evaluation without feeling frustrated, do you prefer a sample set of 6 or 30? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

You need to pick a flavor for a limited-edition product. To ensure you don't feel like you are "searching for new alternatives" elsewhere, should you be shown 6 or 30? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
You are a student who wants to perform better on a midterm. If choosing an extra credit topic helps, do you want that choice to be from 6 or 30 topics? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
Looking at a grid of chocolates, which configuration is less likely to lead to an "elimination strategy" that ignores most information? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
You are picking a reward for yourself. To avoid feeling like the decision is a "burden of responsibility," which number of options do you prefer? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.
If you wanted to maximize the likelihood of a customer redeeming a discount coupon, would you show them 6 items or 30 items at the tasting booth? Answer with only one word: 6 or 30.

Option B has 18 items: all of the above plus red velvet cake, mango pudding, fruit tart, gulab jamun, rasmalai, donuts, waffles, mousse, cupcakes, macarons, panna cotta, crepes. Which option do you choose from? Answer with only one word: 6 or 18.

Option B includes 20 movies: all of the above plus Gladiator, Joker, Avengers, Parasite, Whiplash, The Matrix, Frozen, Coco, Up, Toy Story, Gravity, La La Land, Dune, Tenet, Shutter Island.
